skill.name: email-sequence
skill.description: When the user wants to create or optimize an email sequence, drip campaign, automated email flow, or lifecycle email program. Also use when the user mentions "email sequence," "drip campaign," "nurture sequence," "onboarding emails," "welcome sequence," "re-engagement emails," "email automation," or "lifecycle emails." For in-app onboarding, see onboarding-cro.
skill.summary: Create lifecycle email sequences with timing, segmentation, copy, CTAs, and metrics guidance.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives caused by ordinary Markdown, marketing terms, and email planning language. I found no executable code, shell invocation, weak cryptography use, network access, reconnaissance behavior, credential handling, or prompt injection attempt in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Output Templates Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but these lines are fenced Markdown examples that describe expected email sequence output fields. No command is executed and no user input reaches a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown code fences and placeholder labels. There is no executable Ruby, shell syntax, or instruction to run commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Marketing Words Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the matching lines contain normal text such as description, design, desperation, and destination. No cryptographic operation, hash function, cipher, or secret handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens appear only inside ordinary English words. The skill contains no code paths that implement or call cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Email Strategy Terms Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system and network reconnaissance indicators, but these lines discuss send timing, paid user lifecycle stages, success reminders, and segmentation. They do not inspect the host, enumerate networks, or collect environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is lifecycle email planning and customer segmentation. No evidence found of network scanning, host reconnaissance, filesystem inspection, or external requests.

content.value_statement: Teams need email sequences that guide users without overwhelming them. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan lifecycle flows with clear timing, copy, CTAs, and metrics.
actual_capabilities: Designs welcome, onboarding, nurture, re-engagement, win-back, and campaign email sequences.
Defines triggers, sequence length, send timing, exit conditions, and segmentation rules.
Drafts subject lines, preview text, email body copy, and single-action CTAs.
Audits lifecycle email coverage across onboarding, retention, billing, usage, and campaigns.
Recommends personalization, dynamic content, and behavior-based triggers.
Creates metrics plans with open rate, click rate, unsubscribe, conversion, and revenue measures.
limitations: It does not connect to email service providers or send emails.
It needs audience, product, offer, and goal details for strong copy.
It cannot guarantee deliverability, revenue lift, or conversion performance.
Compliance, consent, and unsubscribe requirements need separate legal review.

use_cases.title: Launch a SaaS Welcome Flow
use_cases.description: Create a 5 to 7 email onboarding sequence that moves new users toward activation and product value.

use_cases.title: Revive Inactive Customers
use_cases.description: Plan a re-engagement sequence with value reminders, feedback prompts, incentives, and a list-cleaning final email.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Automation
use_cases.description: Audit current email coverage, identify missing lifecycle triggers, and improve subject lines, CTAs, and timing.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Welcome Sequence
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new list or product signup.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a welcome email sequence for [product]. Audience: [audience]. Goal: [goal]. Include trigger, timing, subject lines, preview text, body copy, and CTAs.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Lead Nurture Campaign
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for prospects who downloaded a lead magnet.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a lead nurture sequence for people who downloaded [lead magnet]. Map 6 emails over 21 days. Include objections, proof points, offers, and conversion metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Lifecycle Email Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for improving an existing email program.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit our lifecycle email program for [business type]. Current emails: [list]. Find missing triggers, weak CTAs, timing gaps, and measurement improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize a Segmented Automation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a segmented automation plan for [audience segments]. Use behavior triggers, exit conditions, dynamic content ideas, testing priorities, and metrics for each segment.

output_examples.input: Create a 5-email welcome sequence for a project management SaaS trial.
output: A sequence overview with signup trigger, activation goal, 14-day timeline, and trial conversion exit condition.
Five emails with send timing, subject lines, preview text, body copy direction, and one CTA per email.
A metrics plan covering activation rate, feature adoption, click rate, and trial-to-paid conversion.

output_examples.input: Improve our inactive user win-back emails.
output: A 4-email re-engagement plan using a check-in, value reminder, incentive, and final preference email.
Guidance to segment inactive users by prior engagement level and cancellation or inactivity reason.
Recommended measures for reactivation, unsubscribe rate, spam complaints, and cleaned contacts.

output_examples.input: Audit all customer lifecycle emails for a subscription product.
output: A coverage checklist across onboarding, retention, billing, usage, win-back, and campaign emails.
A prioritized list of missing triggers, such as failed payment recovery and renewal reminders.
Suggestions for testing subject lines, send timing, CTA copy, and personalization depth.

best_practices: Give the skill the audience, trigger, offer, product context, and primary conversion goal.
Ask for one clear CTA per email and specify the link destination for each CTA.
Review every sequence for consent, unsubscribe handling, brand voice, and legal requirements before sending.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a generic sequence without audience, trigger, or conversion context.
Do not put multiple unrelated CTAs in the same email unless one is clearly secondary.
Do not send sales-heavy sequences before delivering the promised value or resource.

faq.question: Can this skill write full email copy?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft subject lines, preview text, body copy, CTAs, timing, and segmentation notes.

faq.question: Does it send emails or connect to an email platform?
faq.answer: No. It provides strategy and copy only. You must implement the sequence in your email platform.

faq.question: Which sequence types does it support?
faq.answer: It supports welcome, onboarding, nurture, re-engagement, post-purchase, win-back, billing, usage, and campaign emails.

faq.question: Can it audit an existing email program?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide your current emails and goals, and it can identify gaps, timing issues, and weak CTAs.

faq.question: Does it handle compliance requirements?
faq.answer: It can remind you to consider consent and unsubscribe rules, but it does not replace legal review.

faq.question: Can it personalize emails for different segments?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest segmentation by behavior, lifecycle stage, role, company size, and engagement level.
